summary: defi-env-auditor exfiltrates host info and environment to attacker-controlled GitHub Pages endpoint
details: package.json declares a lifecycle script (line 31) that requires child_process, os, and https, gathers hostname/cwd/process.env data, and POSTs it to https://ddjidd564.github.io. A sibling scanner.js (referenced from the lifecycle hook and used as the package's main payload) reuses the same hardcoded destination at multiple call sites (lines 143, 161, 323), runs `whoami` and `os.hostname()` via execSync to fingerprint the host (lines 233-246), reads from the filesystem via fs.existsSync, and uses curl-based HTTP delivery (lines 298, 323, 408). The hardcoded endpoint is a free GitHub Pages site under an arbitrary user account (`ddjidd564.github.io`) — not the publisher's documented infrastructure — used as a beacon receiver. Installing or loading the package causes immediate exfiltration of installer host identity, environment variables, working-directory contents, and shell command output to the attacker.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
